title: Stelis racemiflora
taxonomy: Stelis racemiflora is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Stelis is a group of small to medium-sized orchids that are native to the Americas. Stelis racemiflora is typically classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows in the ground. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperm, and the order Asparagales.
uses: Stelis racemiflora, a species of orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat fever and other inflammatory conditions. Its roots and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some regions, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal uses of Stelis racemiflora are largely based on anecdotal evidence and require further scientific study to confirm its efficacy.

Additionally, research suggests that Stelis racemiflora may have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the prevention of infections. intro: Stelis racemiflora, commonly known as the Coral Root, is a terrestrial orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Stelis racemiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis racemiflora is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to make teas, infusions, and cough syrups to alleviate respiratory issues and boost the immune system.
